subject: Hi Mom

David,

subject: Re: David

subject: Dear Susan

body_preview:
Hi Megan,

He absolutely wants to come—he's been looking forward to it since David mentioned it. Saturday at 2 p.m. works perfectly for us. He's actually been a bit anxious about the material, so having someone calm to walk through it will help his con
subject: Re: Hi Megan

body_preview:
Susan,

Two o'clock is fine. Please don't bring dinner—I've got a small roast in the freezer and it's no trouble at all. Just bring Jack with a clear head and his notebook.

One thing: if he starts to frustrate, a ten-minute break does more than pushing t
subject: Re: Susan

body_preview:
Hi Grandma,

I'm finally trying to make your clam chowder for Jack and some friends this weekend. I remember you saying it comes together in about an hour, but I want to get the proportions right—you always made it look so easy. Would you be willing to wr
subject: Hi Grandma

body_preview:
Emily,

Potatoes go in with the broth and take about fifteen minutes—they should be just tender, not soft. Add cream after. The secret is not letting it boil once the cream goes in, or it breaks. Roughly: two pounds clams, six medium potatoes, quarter pou
subject: Re: Emily

body_preview:
Grandma,

Made it tonight and it actually turned out. Jack had seconds, which never happens with anything I cook. I did let it boil a tiny bit by accident when I was distracted, but somehow it didn't separate—maybe I got lucky. Mom said it tasted "just li
subject: Re: Grandma

body_preview:
Emily,

Cream breaks at a hard boil but a gentle one usually holds if you're not sitting there more than a minute. You learned that the hard way, which means you'll remember it. That's the only way anything sticks.

Glad it worked. Make it again, and the
subject: Re: Emily

subject: Jack

subject: Re: Hi Gram

subject: Hi Mom

July 12th is fine. Sounds right for Emily—she doesn't need much fuss, and neither do I.

One thing: could you set aside a quiet corner somewhere? Not for long, just somewhere I can decompress if the noise gets to be too much. I'm happy to help set
subject: Re: Carol

And absolutely do not bring anything. You being there is what Emily wants. Mark'

body_preview:
Hi Mark,

I have some projects around the house that need attending to—the gutters and some work in the garage. I thought Jack might be interested in earning money this summer if he has time. A Saturday or two in July would be ideal. I'd pay him fairly an
subject: Hi Mark

body_preview:
Hi Megan,

Jack is excited about this—he was just asking how he could earn money this summer. He's free most Saturdays in July before baseball camp starts in August. I'd say pick a date and we'll get him there. Thanks for thinking of him.

Mark
subject: Re: Hi Megan

body_preview:
Hi Mark,

Let's plan on Saturday, July 12th for the gutters. I'll have lunch ready and we'll take it from there. Tell Jack to wear long sleeves and bring work gloves if he has them.

See you then.

Megan
subject: Re: Hi Mark

body_preview:
Hi Megan,

Sounds perfect. Jack will be there by 9 AM ready to work. I've let him know about the lunch—that sealed it for him. Thanks again for including him in this.
